heroDesc: Repeatable molding for complex plastic parts
bodyTitle: Injection Molding from DFM and tool trial to production
bodyDesc: Injection molding is suitable for plastic parts with production demand. Key factors include material shrinkage, uniform wall thickness, draft angle, gate location and mold cooling. The ranges below are typical references; actual precision depends on resin, part size, tool design and inspection datum.

panels.title: Design Focus
panels.subtitle: Wall / Draft
panels.body: Uniform wall thickness and proper radii help reduce sink, warp and trapped gas.

panels.title: Tool Stage
panels.subtitle: Trial Correction
panels.body: T0/T1 samples verify dimensions, appearance and assembly.

panels.title: Production Stability
panels.subtitle: Process Control
panels.body: Production focuses on molding parameters, material batches and first-article checks.

tableRows: Affected by shrinkage and part size
Abrupt thickness changes may cause sink or warp
Texture or deep cavities need more draft
ABS, PC, PP, PA and more
Refined by resin grade and trial results

steps.title: DFM and material review
steps.body: Check walls, undercuts, parting lines, gates and cosmetic surfaces.

steps.title: Tool design and build
steps.body: Design cavity, cooling, ejection and venting structures.

steps.title: Tool trial and correction
steps.body: Use T0/T1 samples to verify dimensions, appearance and assembly risks.

steps.title: Production and inspection
steps.body: Stabilize parameters and perform first-article, patrol and packaging checks.

asideCta.title: Need a Manufacturing Plan?
asideCta.body: After drawings are submitted, engineers can review geometry, material and volume to suggest a suitable process route.

related.title: Related Process Recommendations
related.desc: Depending on geometry, material and delivery stage, other processes can be combined into a complete manufacturing flow.

items.title: CNC Machining
items.body: Plastic prototypes for fit and assembly checks.

items.title: Surface Finishing
items.body: Supports painting, printing and texture appearance.

faq.title: Injection Molding FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Injection Molding suitable for?
items.a: Injection Molding is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Injection Molding quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Injection Molding?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
